changelog.title: What's new in Google Analytics - Analytics Help
changelog.text:
What's new in Google Analytics - Analytics Help
Skip to main content
Analytics Help
Sign in
Send feedback on...
This help content & information
General Help Center experience
Next
Help Center
Community
Announcements
Analytics
Contact us
Get started with Analytics
Collect and manage data
Report and explore
Advertising and attribution
Audiences and remarketing
Manage accounts, properties, and users
Google Analytics 360
Policies and data privacy
What's new in Google Analytics
This article provides information on the latest releases in Google Analytics for this year. For information on releases from past years, see the
What's New archive
.
Releases
May 13, 2026
May 4, 2026
April 29, 2026
February 10, 2026
January 16, 2026
May 13, 2026
New AI Assistant traffic measurement
Google Analytics now provides a dedicated way to measure and analyze traffic originating from popular AI assistants. You can now identify how users are discovering your site through chatbots like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude via a new
AI Assistant
channel in your Default Channel Group reports.
This update introduces the following changes to your traffic source dimensions:
Medium
: A new "ai-assistant" value is automatically assigned when the referrer matches a recognized AI Assistant
Channel Group
: These visits are categorized under the "AI Assistant" channel
Campaign
: Traffic from these sources will be identified with the "(ai-assistant)" campaign name
This feature helps you monitor how generative AI impacts your business by tracking user clicks, trending AI sources, and how this traffic compares to traditional channels like organic search.
May 4, 2026
Conversion support in the Data API
This feature may not be available to your Google Analytics property. The Google Analytics team is actively working to expand this feature to more properties. Please reach out to your support team if you have questions about the eligibility of your property.
Cross-channel
conversion reporting data
is now available from the Google Analytics Data API in alpha for Google Analytics and Google Ads customers. Google Analytics developers can now gain programmatic access to the same paid and organic reporting data that's available in the Analytics advertising section's Conversion performance report. Refer to the
Google Analytics Data API changelog
for more details about this new API surface.
April 29, 2026
Configure your Google Analytics settings using Task Assistant
To help you get the most out of your Google Analytics property, we are introducing Task Assistant. Task Assistant provides tailored recommendations to help you optimize your configuration and improve data collection,. Available from the left navigation menu, it organizes these recommendations into actionable categories, such as connecting your accounts, enhancing your reporting, and fixing data issues. You can easily manage your property's progress by marking tasks as complete as you finish them, or skipping tasks that aren't relevant to your current business goals. Return back to the Task Assistant for additional tasks.
Learn more about Task Assistant
February 10, 2026
Generated insights on the Home page
We’ve added
Generated insights
to your Home page. This feature summarizes the top three data changes since your last visit, including key configuration updates, anomalies, and seasonality trends.This helps you quickly catch up on what’s happening in your property and spot performance shifts without needing to dig into detailed reports.
Learn more about Generated insights
Cross-channel budgeting (Beta)
This feature may not be available to your Google Analytics property. The Google Analytics team is actively working to expand this feature to more properties. Please reach out to your support team if you have questions about the eligibility of your property.
The Google Analytics team is excited to announce the release of cross-channel budgeting. Use cross-channel budgeting to track performance and optimize paid channel investments.
January 16, 2026
Cross-channel budgeting (Beta)
This feature may not be available to your Google Analytics property. The Google Analytics team is actively working to expand this feature to more properties. Please reach out to your support team if you have questions about the eligibility of your property.
The Google Analytics team is excited to announce the release of cross-channel budgeting. Use cross-channel budgeting to track performance and optimize paid channel investments.
Projection plans
help you identify areas for improvement and make quick optimization decisions. You can see how advertising channels are expected to perform against your performance indicators (KPIs) like spend, conversions, and revenue.
Scenario plans
help you determine the optimal distribution of your budget for future media initiatives by letting you explore potential ROI at different budget levels.
With these tools, you'll be able to answer critical questions like, "Is my spend on track?", "How many future conversions are forecasted based on my planned spend?" and “How should I allocate my budgets to maximize revenue and ROI?”
Learn more about cross-channel budgeting
Improved web conversion management and reporting capabilities for Google Ads customers (Beta)
This feature may not be available to your Google Analytics property. The Google Analytics team is actively working to expand this feature to more properties. Please reach out to your support team if you have questions about the eligibility of your property.
More flexible web conversion management tools and new cross-channel reporting features are now available for marketers and advertisers.
Conversion attribution settings are now adjustable independently for every conversion, enabling customers to fine-tune their bidding strategies in Google Ads, eliminate a common cause of conversion reporting discrepancies versus Google Ads, and gain greater confidence in the efficacy of their ad spend.
We’re also adding more comprehensive cross-channel reporting features and capabilities, including new reporting dimension filters, to unlock new insights.
Learn more about cross-channel conversion reporting in Analytics
Conversion attribution analysis report (Beta)
This feature may not be available to your Google Analytics property. The Google Analytics team is actively working to expand this feature to more properties. Please reach out to your support team if you have questions about the eligibility of your property.
The
Conversion attribution analysis
report is a new addition to the advertising workspace that provides customers with the ability to uncover the full value of their marketing channels across the entire customer journey.
This report helps you visualize how different touchpoints contribute to your bottom line through two specialized views:
Assisted conversions (Last Click)
:
This view allows you to see "Assists"—touchpoints that engaged customers early in their journey but weren't the final click. This is essential for identifying undervalued upper-funnel channels, such as YouTube or Demand Gen, that drive interest before a final search or direct visit.
Refined funnel analysis (Data-driven attribution)
:
This view categorizes touchpoints into Early, Mid, and Late stages. We now separate single-touchpoint paths from multi-touchpoint journeys, giving you a clearer picture of which campaigns are closing complex journeys versus those that act as standalone drivers.
By using these new insights, advertisers can better justify upper-funnel spend and build a more holistic budget strategy that rewards every stage of the funnel.
Give feedback about this article
Choose a section to give feedback on
Was this helpful?
How can we improve it?
Submit
Need more help?
Try these next steps:
Post to the help community
Get answers from community members
Contact us
Tell us more and we’ll help you get there

Google Analytics

Start learning about
Google Analytics
Google Analytics is the go-to platform for millions of website and app owners seeking to gain a deeper understanding of their website and app performance. With Google Analytics, you can fine-tune your digital strategy, optimize your campaigns, and take your online presence to new heights.
Choose your path
New to Google Analytics?
Check out our
free courses
to get started.
Looking for developer documentation?
See
Google Analytics for developers
.
Choose your path
Analytics for beginners and small businesses
For anyone who's new to Google Analytics or simply needs to understand its fundamentals.
Get started
Analytics for marketing specialists and digital analysts
For marketers or data analysts looking to explore Google Analytics, including advanced features and capabilities.
Get started
Analytics for developers
For developers who want to tag a website or app, set up events or ecommerce, or build custom Analytics functionality.
Get started
Take the Analytics Academy course on Skillshop
Google provides free courses to help you get started with Google Analytics. First, learn how Google Analytics works and create your account and property. Then, learn how to navigate the Google Analytics web interface, use reports for your business, and manage the data you send.
Get started
Watch Google Analytics videos
Get started with Google Analytics
Learn how to set up Google Analytics on a website for the first time using Google Tag Manager.
Turn insights into ROI with Google Analytics
Learn how Google Analytics helps you uncover relevant customer insights, that you can quickly activate in Google’s media platforms to drive performance.
Best practices for account structure
Learn the best practice methods for setting up your Google Analytics account structure, including the use of Subproperties and Roll-up properties.
Watch more on the Google Analytics YouTube
  channel →
Continue learning
What's New
Learn about new features and functionality in the release notes for Google Analytics.
Discord
Ask Google Analytics questions to a community of practitioners to help accomplish your data collection and analysis goals.
Ask an expert
Find Certified Companies and Sales Partners who can support you every step of the way.
Learn about Google Analytics 360
Learn with GMP
Learn more about Google Analytics 360 by attending a live course with subject matter experts offered in US, Canada, and Latin America time zones.
GMP Academy
Learn more about Google Analytics 360 by attending a live course with subject matter experts in Europe, Middle East, and Africa time zones.
Enterprise Marketing Portal
Are you a certified GMP Partner? Visit the EMP for exclusive partner-only resources to deepen your Google Analytics knowledge.
Developer resources
Developer documentation
Learn how to use Google Analytics APIs and developer products to automate your measurement and reporting solutions.
Sample code
Get started with Google Analytics for developers using our client libraries and code samples on GitHub.
Demos and Tools
Discover what's possible with the Google Analytics platform using our demos and tools for developers.
Didn't find what you were looking for?
Search the Google Analytics documentation to find what you're looking for.

integrations.title: Work with Partners
integrations.text:
Work with Partners
Jump to Content
Enterprise Marketing Portal
Home
Become a Partner
Work with Partners
FAQ
Sign in
menu
Enterprise Marketing Portal
Work with Partners
Whether you’re looking for help with a single project or a bigger, long-term partnership, Google's ecosystem of partners and experts have the resources and expertise you need to help you achieve more with your marketing.
Google Marketing Partners
Advertising Technology Partners
YouTube Activation Partners
Marketing Partner Gallery
Find a Google Marketing Platform Partner
Advertising Technology Partner directory
Find an Advertising Technology Partner
YouTube Activation Partner Roster
Find a YouTube Activation Partner
Expert Help, When You Need It
Expertise
Partners are vetted by Google so you can trust you're working with a company who knows our solutions and can provide the expertise you are looking for.
info_outline
Flexibility
Our partners offer a broad array of products, services and multiple engagement models — including self-service, full-service and hybrid — to meet the specific needs of your business.
info_outline
A Full Toolbox
Whether you're looking for help with strategy, media management, system integrations, measurement and attribution or more — our partners offer the right combination of tools, expertise, services and products.
info_outline
Google Marketing Platform Partners
The Expertise That You Need
Whether you're looking for help with a single project or a bigger, long-term partnership, Google Marketing Platform Partners have the resources and expertise you need to help you achieve more with your marketing.
Look for the Badge
When a company has a partners badge, it means they've been carefully vetted and meet our standards. It also means we consider them to be a trusted business partner in providing the kind of support you need to be successful.
Find a Partner’s Specialization
Partners specialize in one or more of the following areas. Explore our partners to find one that's right for you.
Display and Video Advertising
Need help with your media strategy, planning, buying, trafficking, and optimization including programmatic buys across display and video? These partners are the experts who can help.
info_outline
Search Advertising
These partners create and optimize search and other performance channels including keyword strategy and budget planning.
info_outline
Analytics and Testing
These partners provide data strategy, data collection, testing, reporting, and dashboard services for websites and mobile apps.
info_outline
Creative Design
These partners are creative experts and provide services to support your display and video advertising creative needs.
info_outline
Explore the Gallery for Google Marketing Partners
Whatever your needs are, we have Certified Companies and Sales Partners who can support you every step of the way.
Explore the Gallery
open_in_new
Advertising Technology Partners
Achieve Your Goals
The Advertising Technology Partners website and directory provides information on independent companies that have advertising integrations with Google. Whether you’re looking to maximize reach, build consideration and interest, or drive outcomes, these partners can help you meet your business goals on Google media.*
About the Program
The Advertising Technology Partners program includes select third-party ad measurement partners and product categories available on YouTube, Google Video Partners, and Search inventory, and is not a complete list of partners that support measurement across Google media. Additionally, new product categories may be added over time.
*Information in the directory is provided for reference only, and is subject to change. Advertising Technology Partners provide independent measurement solutions on Google media. As such, Google is not responsible for and makes no warranties or guarantees about (i) any third-party company, product, or information listed in the directory, or (ii) the coverage or availability of any third-party product on Google inventory (e.g. for a certain inventory type or ad format, in a given country, etc.). Inclusion in the program and directory is not an endorsement from Google. Please conduct your own due diligence of any company that you choose to work with.
Find a Partner to Support Measurement
Advertising Technology Partners have an integration with Google for one or more of the following ad measurement product categories.
For more information about these product category integrations, please refer to the general integration information
here
.
Brand Lift
Brand Lift partners measure the effectiveness of a YouTube brand
           campaign by surveying an audience of users who were either exposed
            to ads in a campaign, or eligible but not exposed. The survey quantifies
            the incremental change that the campaign had on brand perception and
            user behavior. Key metrics include Lift in Awareness, Consideration
            and Purchase intent, Cost Per Lifted User, Total Reach and Frequency.
Brand Safety
Brand Safety partners provide independent verification and transparency
            into the brand safety and suitability of ad placements on YouTube
            and Google Video Partners inventory, based on global industry standards
            defined by the Global Alliance for Responsible Media (“GARM”).
            Key metrics include GARM brand safety and GARM brand suitability.
Enhanced Conversions for Web
Enhanced conversions for web can improve the accuracy of your conversion
            measurement on YouTube and Search to unlock more powerful bidding.
            It supplements your existing conversion tags by sending hashed first party conversion data from your website to Google in a privacy safe way. Enhanced conversions for web partners can streamline implementation and troubleshoot setups through additional technical support.
Geo Experiments (GeoX)
GeoX partners help advertisers understand the return on their investments
            across YouTube, Google Video Partners, and Search inventory by measuring
            the impact of media on driving incremental offline sales volume leveraging
            small and controlled geo-based tests under live conditions. Key metrics
            reported include % lift in sales, incremental sales and return on ad spend (ROAS).
Reach
Ad Reach partners provide advertisers and agencies with independent measurement of the audience and reach of their cross-media advertising campaigns on YouTube and Google Video Partners inventory. Key metrics include impressions (inclusive of co-viewing), unique audience reach, frequency, and on-target %.
User Sales Lift
User Sales Lift partners measure how effective YouTube campaigns are at moving CPG products off the shelves, helping brand advertisers optimize their spend based on performance insights. Key metrics reported include % lift in sales, incremental sales and return on ad spend (ROAS).
Viewability and Invalid Traffic
Viewability and Invalid Traffic partners provide independent and transparent measurement of video and display inventory on YouTube and Google Video Partners sites. Key metrics include viewability, audibility, watch time, and fraud/bot detection, based on global industry standards and proprietary vendor methodologies.
Explore the Directory for Advertising Technology Partners
Explore the directory to learn more about Google’s Advertising Technology Partners. When a company is listed in the Advertising Technology Partners directory, it means that they have an integration with Google to support your marketing objectives for one or more of the product categories above, and that they are a member of this Advertising Technology Partners program.
Explore the Directory
open_in_new
YouTube Activation Partners
Discover YouTube Advertising Experts
Whether you need strategic guidance to evolve your YouTube buying strategy, or additional expertise to manage your next YouTube campaign, YouTube Activation Partners can help you achieve your advertising goals.
Work with an Expert
When a company appears on our YouTube Activation Partner roster, it means they've been reviewed by Google and meet our standards.
The YouTube Activation Partners badge means we consider them to be a trusted business partner for both Advertisers and Agencies looking for specialized YouTube support & expertise.
Explore YouTube Activation Partners
From planning to execution and beyond, our partners are ready to help you activate on YouTube.
Explore the Roster
open_in_new
Interested in Becoming a YouTube Activation Partner?
While we are not onboarding new partners at this time, we are accepting applications for future membership on a rolling basis. Before you apply, please make sure you review all the requirements you must meet in order to be considered for future membership.
Expand all
unfold_more
Admission Requirements
Below is some guidance about the criteria that companies should meet
          to be considered for YouTube Activation Partner Program:
Company is an independent service provider servicing end advertising clients.
          For clarity, company must not be not partially owned, nor a subsidiary of, a Media Agency HoldCo.
Company has an established and scaled Google Ads business, with material managed YouTube spend in Google Ads annually.
Company has a dedicated sales team to commercialize video-based media activation solutions in market, and account management capabilities to provide support to joining customers.
Company has dedicated engineering / development resources to integrate with Google / YT product integrations (i.e. APIs, etc).
Company has strong customer demand for company’s Google-centric services (Note: Google assesses demand in a few ways, including sponsorship from major brands and agencies, advertiser inquiries provided to Google Sales, general market research, proven adoption in a target market).
Company is in full compliance with Google & YouTube policies and have accounts in good standing.
Agrees to have core team members complete Google Ads Video certification.
Apply
open_in_new
Take the Next Step
All users are welcome to sign up as a Member of our EMP community!
Become a Member
Privacy
Terms
About Google
Google Products

analysis.name: Google Analytics
analysis.tagline: Fine-tune your digital strategy, optimize your campaigns, and take your online presence to new heights.
analysis.value_proposition: A free web and app analytics platform that helps businesses understand traffic sources, user behavior, and conversion performance to optimize marketing spend.
analysis.ideal_customer: Website and app owners, digital marketers, and data analysts at businesses of any size seeking to measure and act on online audience behavior

features.name: website traffic tracking
features.description: Measures sessions, users, pageviews, and engagement across web properties
features.evidence_quote: Google Analytics is the go-to platform for millions of website and app owners seeking to gain a deeper understanding of their website and app performance.

features.name: app performance tracking
features.description: Measures user behavior and performance within mobile and web applications
features.evidence_quote: millions of website and app owners seeking to gain a deeper understanding of their website and app performance

features.name: ai assistant traffic measurement
features.description: Dedicated channel group identifying traffic from AI chatbots like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude
features.evidence_quote: identify how users are discovering your site through chatbots like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude via a new AI Assistant channel

features.name: default channel group reports
features.description: Categorizes traffic into named channels (organic, paid, AI assistant, etc.) for side-by-side comparison
features.evidence_quote: These visits are categorized under the 'AI Assistant' channel

features.name: traffic source dimensions
features.description: Tracks medium, campaign, and channel group dimensions for every session
features.evidence_quote: Medium: A new 'ai-assistant' value is automatically assigned when the referrer matches a recognized AI Assistant

features.name: data api
features.description: Programmatic REST API for querying analytics reporting data
features.evidence_quote: Google Analytics developers can now gain programmatic access to the same paid and organic reporting data

features.name: cross-channel conversion reporting
features.description: Unified paid and organic conversion data accessible via the Data API (alpha)
features.evidence_quote: Cross-channel conversion reporting data is now available from the Google Analytics Data API in alpha

features.name: task assistant
features.description: Guided in-product configuration recommendations organized into actionable categories
features.evidence_quote: Task Assistant provides tailored recommendations to help you optimize your configuration and improve data collection

features.name: generated insights
features.description: AI-generated summaries on the home page highlighting top data changes since last visit
features.evidence_quote: This feature summarizes the top three data changes since your last visit, including key configuration updates, anomalies, and seasonality trends.

features.name: anomaly detection
features.description: Automatically surfaces statistical anomalies in traffic and conversion data
features.evidence_quote: summarizes the top three data changes since your last visit, including key configuration updates, anomalies, and seasonality trends

features.name: cross-channel budgeting
features.description: Tracks paid channel performance and spend against KPIs across channels (beta)
features.evidence_quote: Use cross-channel budgeting to track performance and optimize paid channel investments.

features.name: projection plans
features.description: Forecasts how advertising channels will perform against KPIs like spend, conversions, and revenue
features.evidence_quote: Projection plans help you identify areas for improvement and make quick optimization decisions.

features.name: scenario plans
features.description: Models optimal budget allocation across channels at different spending levels to maximize ROI
features.evidence_quote: Scenario plans help you determine the optimal distribution of your budget for future media initiatives

features.name: conversion attribution settings
features.description: Per-conversion attribution model configuration to align with Google Ads bidding strategies
features.evidence_quote: Conversion attribution settings are now adjustable independently for every conversion, enabling customers to fine-tune their bidding strategies

features.name: web conversion management
features.description: Flexible tools for defining, managing, and reporting on web conversion events
features.evidence_quote: More flexible web conversion management tools and new cross-channel reporting features are now available for marketers and advertisers.

features.name: google ads integration
features.description: Bidirectional data sharing with Google Ads for conversion import and audience export
features.evidence_quote: Cross-channel conversion reporting data is now available...for Google Analytics and Google Ads customers

features.name: subproperties
features.description: Create scoped child properties from a parent property for segmented reporting
features.evidence_quote: Learn the best practice methods for setting up your Google Analytics account structure, including the use of Subproperties

features.name: roll-up properties
features.description: Aggregate data across multiple properties into a single unified reporting view
features.evidence_quote: Learn the best practice methods for setting up your Google Analytics account structure, including the use of...Roll-up properties.

features.name: google tag manager integration
features.description: Tag website or app for Analytics collection via Google Tag Manager without code changes
features.evidence_quote: Learn how to set up Google Analytics on a website for the first time using Google Tag Manager.

features.name: event tracking
features.description: Capture and report on custom user interaction events on websites and apps
features.evidence_quote: For developers who want to tag a website or app, set up events or ecommerce

features.name: ecommerce tracking
features.description: Track product views, add-to-cart, purchases, and revenue from online stores
features.evidence_quote: For developers who want to tag a website or app, set up events or ecommerce

features.name: audience management
features.description: Build and manage audience segments based on behavior for remarketing and analysis
features.evidence_quote: Audiences and remarketing

features.name: remarketing audiences
features.description: Export audiences to Google Ads and other platforms for targeted ad campaigns
features.evidence_quote: Audiences and remarketing

features.name: advertising attribution reporting
features.description: Multi-touch attribution modeling for paid and organic media channels
features.evidence_quote: Advertising and attribution

features.name: mcp server
features.description: Model Context Protocol server enabling AI assistant access to Analytics data
features.evidence_quote: Try the MCP server for Google Analytics. Install from GitHub

features.name: measurement protocol / developer api
features.description: HTTP-based protocol and client libraries for sending custom hit data to Analytics
features.evidence_quote: Learn how to use Google Analytics APIs and developer products to automate your measurement and reporting solutions.

features.name: custom analytics functionality
features.description: Build bespoke measurement solutions using client libraries and code samples
features.evidence_quote: For developers who want to...build custom Analytics functionality.

features.name: enhanced conversions for web
features.description: Privacy-safe hashed first-party conversion data sent to Google to improve measurement accuracy
features.evidence_quote: sending hashed first party conversion data from your website to Google in a privacy safe way

features.name: geo experiments (geox)
features.description: Geographic holdout experiments to measure incremental return on advertising investment
features.evidence_quote: GeoX partners help advertisers understand the return on their investment

features.name: brand lift measurement
features.description: Survey-based measurement of campaign impact on brand awareness and purchase intent (via partners)
features.evidence_quote: Brand Lift partners measure the effectiveness of a YouTube brand campaign by surveying an audience

features.name: brand safety measurement
features.description: Third-party verification of ad placement suitability against GARM standards (via partners)
features.evidence_quote: Brand Safety partners provide independent verification and transparency into the brand safety and suitability of ad placements

features.name: google analytics 360
features.description: Enterprise-grade Analytics tier with higher data limits, SLAs, and advanced features
features.evidence_quote: Learn about Google Analytics 360

features.name: skillshop / free courses
features.description: Free structured training curriculum covering Analytics fundamentals through advanced use
features.evidence_quote: Google provides free courses to help you get started with Google Analytics.

features.name: certified partner ecosystem
features.description: Vetted agency and technology partners for strategy, implementation, and reporting services
features.evidence_quote: Partners are vetted by Google so you can trust you're working with a company who knows our solutions

features.name: account and property management
features.description: Multi-account hierarchy with role-based user access control
features.evidence_quote: Manage accounts, properties, and users

differentiators: Native bidirectional integration with Google Ads for conversion import and audience activation
AI Assistant channel group automatically categorizing traffic from ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude
Free tier available with no seat-based pricing for standard properties
MCP server for direct AI assistant access to Analytics data
Roll-up and Subproperties for enterprise-scale multi-property aggregation (GA360)
Cross-channel budgeting with projection and scenario planning built into the UI
Massive global partner ecosystem (Google Marketing Platform Partners, Advertising Technology Partners)

recent_ships.what: AI Assistant traffic measurement channel — tracks visits from ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude in Default Channel Group
recent_ships.source: Google Analytics now provides a dedicated way to measure and analyze traffic originating from popular AI assistants.

recent_ships.what: Cross-channel conversion reporting in Data API (alpha) — programmatic access to paid and organic conversion data
recent_ships.source: Cross-channel conversion reporting data is now available from the Google Analytics Data API in alpha

recent_ships.what: Task Assistant — guided configuration recommendations from left nav to optimize property setup
recent_ships.source: Task Assistant provides tailored recommendations to help you optimize your configuration and improve data collection

recent_ships.what: Generated insights on Home page — AI summaries of top 3 data changes since last visit
recent_ships.source: This feature summarizes the top three data changes since your last visit, including key configuration updates, anomalies, and seasonality trends.

recent_ships.what: Cross-channel budgeting beta — projection plans and scenario plans for paid media budget optimization
recent_ships.source: Use cross-channel budgeting to track performance and optimize paid channel investments.

recent_ships.what: Per-conversion attribution settings and improved web conversion management for Google Ads customers (beta)
recent_ships.source: Conversion attribution settings are now adjustable independently for every conversion, enabling customers to fine-tune their bidding strategies

praised: Deep integration with Google Ads ecosystem enables seamless conversion import and audience activation
Free tier is genuinely capable for most SMB use cases
Large training and certification ecosystem (Skillshop) lowers onboarding friction
complaints: Tech-savvy users actively block Google Analytics tracking (HN: 1214 upvotes)
EU regulators declared it illegal — French DPA and Italian watchdog both issued bans (HN: 1172 and 942 upvotes)
Firefox treats GA as a tracker and replaces it with a no-op in strict tracking protection mode
Community appetite for privacy-first alternatives is strong — multiple HN threads about Umami and minimalist replacements
"Stop feeding the beast" sentiment — data monopoly and Google ecosystem lock-in concerns

free_tier.limits: Standard property: up to 10M events/month; no seat caps documented in provided sources

tiers.name: Google Analytics (Standard)
tiers.headline_inclusions: Full standard analytics, reporting, Data API, GA4 events, Google Ads integration

tiers.name: Google Analytics 360
tiers.headline_inclusions: Enterprise SLA, higher event limits, subproperties, roll-up properties, dedicated support

pricing.notes: Pricing page not in provided sources. Standard GA is free with usage limits. GA 360 is enterprise/contact-sales, historically ~$150K+/yr. Cross-channel budgeting and conversion API features currently in beta and may be gated by property eligibility.
